Title: Vanillin formation from ferulic acid in Vanilla planifolia is catalysed by a single enzyme

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Vanilla pod inner tissues; engineered yeast product pool
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Selected from combined vanilla transcriptomic and proteomic datasets from inner pod tissue
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Specifically glucosylates vanillin and enables vanillin glucoside formation when co-expressed with VpVAN in yeast
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate cloning; yeast co-expression with VpScVAN; LC-MS metabolite profiling; in vitro substrate specificity assay cited in Supplementary Fig. 3
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Yeast supplied with ferulic acid or ferulic acid glucoside
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: 454 transcriptome of Vanilla planifolia pod, SRA BioProject SRP023166; targeted proteomics
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful stacking partner with VpVAN to trap toxic/free vanillin as glucoside in engineered platforms
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Produced only low amounts of vanillin glucoside in yeast relative to AtUGT72E2 under some conditions
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VpUGT72U1 was identified from vanilla pod omics datasets as a candidate UGT. 2. It specifically glucosylates vanillin. 3. Co-expression with VpVAN in yeast enabled vanillin glucoside formation from ferulic acid or ferulic acid glucoside.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Broad-specificity UGT that supports relatively higher vanillin glucoside formation together with VpVAN in yeast and is proposed as a partner for plant engineering
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast co-expression with VpScVAN and LC-MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Yeast supplied with ferulic acid or ferulic acid glucoside; molasses-based medium
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Recommended partner gene to co-express with VpVAN in microbes or plants to stabilize vanillin as glucoside and avoid reduction to vanillyl alcohol
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad substrate specificity may cause off-target glucosylation of multiple phenolics
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtUGT72E2 glucosylates vanillin and several related phenolics. 2. Co-expression with VpVAN in yeast gave larger vanillin glucoside accumulation than VpUGT72U1 in some assays. 3. The paper proposes AtUGT72E2 as a practical engineering partner for VpVAN.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Vanilla planifolia; Nicotiana benthamiana; Hordeum vulgare; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Hydratase/lyase-type enzyme with high sequence similarity to papain-like cysteine proteinases/aleurain class; contains ER-targeting signal peptide and cysteine protease-like catalytic motifs
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal chain-shortening step from ferulic acid/ferulic acid glucoside to vanillin/vanillin glucoside
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Directly converts ferulic acid to vanillin and ferulic acid glucoside to vanillin glucoside through a non-oxidative two-carbon chain-shortening hydratase/lyase reaction
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Vanillin is the major vanilla flavor constituent and an antimicrobial/defense compound; glucosylation stores it in a non-toxic form
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Inner part of vanilla pod, especially papillae and placental tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High transcript/protein levels in inner pod tissues; high transcript levels in single cells a few cell layers from the inner epidermis of 6-month-old pods
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In barley transgenics detected in leaves; in heterologous systems activity observed after transient/stable expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes de novo vanillin/vanillin glucoside formation from ferulic acid pathway intermediates; in tobacco and barley leads mainly to vanillyl alcohol glucoside accumulation due to endogenous downstream metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as the first committed and decisive terminal step in vanillin formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Radiolabeled precursor feeding in vanilla pods; candidate-gene identification by transcriptomics/proteomics; coupled in vitro transcription/translation enzyme assay; LC-MS metabolite profiling; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; stable expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae and Hordeum vulgare; in situ PCR localization
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Endogenous alcohol dehydrogenases; endogenous UDP-glucosyltransferases; AtUGT72E2; VpUGT72U1
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing 6-month-old pods; substrate feeding with ferulic acid or ferulic acid glucoside; molasses-based medium in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GPD and TEF promoters in yeast; constitutive ubiquitin promoter in barley
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 454 transcriptome of Vanilla planifolia pod, SRA BioProject SRP023166; targeted proteomics/mass fingerprinting
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for engineering natural vanillin production in microbes and plants; should be stacked with efficient vanillin-glucosyltransferase and with suppression of competing ferulate decarboxylation/reduction routes; useful for marker development in vanilla breeding
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In heterologous plants vanillin is reduced to vanillyl alcohol unless an effective vanillin UGT is co-expressed; in yeast flux is lost to 4-vinylguaiacol via PAD1/FDC1
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Does not metabolize p-coumaric acid, caffeic acid, or their glucosides; previous assignment as 4-HBS could not be confirmed; activity retained without ER signal peptide or propeptide truncation in vitro/yeast

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: VpVAN homolog with 71% amino-acid identity; cysteine proteinase-like hydratase/lyase-type protein
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Strongly inferred ferulic acid/ferulic acid-derived endogenous substrate
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Vanillin, detected indirectly as vanillyl alcohol glucoside in tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Encodes a functional vanillin synthase homolog that supports vanillin formation in heterologous tobacco expression assays
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavor compound and defense-associated phenolic aldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves release trace vanillin in source species; heterologous product detected in tobacco leaves as vanillyl alcohol glucoside
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Confers vanillin-forming activity, evidenced by accumulation of vanillyl alcohol glucoside after transient tobacco expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome identification and transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana followed by LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Endogenous tobacco alcohol dehydrogenases; endogenous tobacco UDP-glucosyltransferases
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in tobacco leaves
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Illumina HiSeq transcriptome from Glechoma hederacea leaves; accession KJ775791
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative vanillin synthase source for metabolic engineering of natural vanillin production
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Observed product in tobacco is mainly vanillyl alcohol glucoside, indicating endogenous reduction/glucosylation may divert free vanillin
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional assignment based on heterologous expression; detailed substrate specificity not directly biochemically characterized in this paper
